champion.narrative: Andrea Biagini (Cost Effectiveness & Purchasing Intelligence, Evaluator) is the only engaged contact and is developing as a coach, not yet a champion. Evidence: Andrea is the operational point of contact, attended the 9 April demo, and re-engaged 25 June with a positive but passive update ('we are currently still bringing forward this project'). However, Andrea has not demonstrated advocacy: no introductions to the Decision Maker (Bogdan Marius N., unengaged), no EB mapping, no internal selling visible, no shared business case or internal docs, no competitive defense (SynerTrade is the incumbent and a named competitor, but no evidence Andrea is pushing Zycus over them). Andrea's engagement cooled for 2.5 months (9 April to 25 June) despite 3 Zycus reminders, suggesting low urgency or lack of internal sponsorship. The relationship is passive and single-threaded. Champion strength: developing, at risk of stalling. The move: over the next 14 days, ask Andrea directly for an intro to Bogdan and for visibility into Pirelli's internal evaluation timeline, then assess whether Andrea will advocate or is simply a conduit. If Andrea cannot or will not facilitate EB/DM access, escalate through the deal owner's manager to Bogdan directly.

competition.narrative: Primary competitor is Do Nothing / Inertia. Pirelli is in market scouting with no committed timeline, no EB, no budget, and a passive re-engagement tone. The 2.5-month cooling period and the buyer's apology ('sorry for not getting back to you, but they were quite busy') signal that this project is not urgent and may stall indefinitely. Secondary competitor is SynerTrade (SF Competitors__c). SynerTrade is Pirelli's existing vendor (incumbency advantage: integrated, known, low switching cost to do nothing). No evidence of an active SynerTrade competitive threat (no mention in calls or next steps of SynerTrade demos, pricing, or roadmap discussions), but inertia defaults to the incumbent. How we win against Do Nothing: build a quantified ROI case (FTE savings, cycle-time reduction, error-rate improvement) with Andrea, escalate it to the EB/CFO, and create urgency by tying the value case to a concrete 2026 implementation milestone (e.g., 'if we start evaluation in July, pilot in Q4, you realize $X savings in 2027'). How we win against SynerTrade: position Merlin (AI negotiation agent) and Agentic AI as the differentiation SynerTrade cannot match, and make AI-driven automation the centerpiece of the use-case demo and ROI workshop. The wedge: Pirelli stated they want AI procurement tools (27 March); SynerTrade is a traditional S2P suite without agentic capabilities. Activation: run a Merlin demo focused on supplier price negotiation and PO automation, quantify the AI ROI, and present it to the EB.
